name: The Unwearable Hat
description: Gives the wearer an overwhelming compulsion to remove the hat from their head.

name: Glowing Shard
description: A bright glowing gem. Personnel are advised not to stare at the shard during nocturnal hours. All experimentation requires a strict release form.

name: The Shapeshifter
description: An entity impersonating a deceased researcher. Persistent in its attempts to convince staff to let it out of containment. Implied to have become Jesus Christ at some point in time.

name: Tub of Blood
description: A large metal tub of blood. Releases an aggressive humanoid entity during the night.

name: Time Brake
description: Slows time locally in a range of 3 feet, getting progressively stronger the closer an object gets.

name: Amnesia Threshold
description: A doorway that removes anywhere from days to years of a person's memory. Constructed from re-used ship materials.

name: The Red Chair
description: A chair that transports the user to Anteverse 19. Formerly in possession of Aleister Crowley.

name: Darkwater Beast
description: Aquatic in nature and repelled by radiation in the x-ray range. Originates from Anteverse 42.

name: The Library
description: A library that contains every possible permutations of a book.

name: The Pool Room
description: IS-0031 is off-limits to all GATE personnel, unless accompanied by tactical TR teams equipped in accordance with manifest GCRF-ISP-0018A, including IR and low-light observation equipment, or approved Gatekeeper staff. Hydrophobic protective wear is encouraged and, pursuant to GISP3.2 provisions, all returning persons must submit to full-spectrum decontamination until further notice.

name: The Lost Dog
description: A dog that appears in 13 oil paintings of the nymph Callisto. The dog is not found in any of the original sketches of the paintings.

name: Gravity Dampener
description: Reduces gravity in a localized area. Keep away from perforations and gravitationally sensitive equipment.

name: Kizz Cola
description: A soda obtained from a strange vending machine. Effects on consumption vary. Neurological effects are under study. Consumption not recommended.

name: Reaper
description: An entity that is able to enter and exit locations at will, as well as transport individuals to Anteverse 39. Stunned or temporarily dispersed by high-energy electromagnetic radiation.

name: Secret Project
description: All paperwork regarding IS-0060 must be filed in Sector 13. Security Officers must log the full name of the person who found it, as well as when they were made aware of it, and where the paperwork was found.

name: Composer
description: Gigantic, skeletal creatures that emit sound from their chests. Made from a material similar to porcelain, only far more durable. Lacks any sense of hearing. Originates from Anteverse 1.

name: The Earworm
description: An unidentifiable song that infects the minds of listeners. Gradually causes insanity.

name: Space Queen
description: An arcade cabinet that can teleport the player into the game reality.

name: Scuttlebug
description: A small, blind, skittish creature commonly seen in Anteverse 2 B. Hides in burrows when startled by sound.

name: Leyak
description: A creature that hunts a singular person at a time, preventing others besides the prey from seeing it. If encountered, stare directly at it until it vanishes.

name: Krasue
description: A variant of IS-0091 that only appears at extreme low temperatures.

name: The Lodestones
description: Attracts and tethers perforation gateways.

name: Belief Virus
description: A virus that only affects those who believe it exists.

name: The Creeping Crystal
description: A constantly growing crystal. A harvesting system is in place to cull the growth. If this system fails for longer than 36 hours, incinerate the subject.

name: Mysterious Fish
description: [REDACTED]

name: Antique Shotgun
description: Previously displayed anomalous effects. The weapon is to be considered highly dangerous.

name: The Sun Disk
description: Data access is impossible. Extremely hazardous. Suspected to be in possession of The Order.

name: The Love Potion
description: Horrific effects on mammalian lifeforms. If personnel are exposed to the pathogen, they are to be prevented from leaving. Live fire is authorized.

name: The Time Bomb
description: An impervious object that consistently grows by 0.1% every year with no signs of stopping. In 40,000 years, this could prove to be quite a threat. Has been launched into space as of 1985. Originates from Anteverse 7A.

name: The Minder
description: Telepathically lures food, imparting a 'Feeling of wellness' to anything in an immediate area. Originates from Anteverse 21.

name: The Wayseeker
description: Considered a godlike figure by the inhabitants of Anteverse 2. Believed to have created the life forms of that anteverse. Currently contained at the Intrados Facility.

name: Tarasque
description: A microbe from Anteverse 42 that has grown to immense size in our universe.

name: Behemoth
description: A larger, red variant of IS-0121.

name: Shadowhopper
description: Research on the subject is to be done remotely. Personnel are not permitted to enter the containment unit. Movement requires a full team of Gatekeepers.

name: The Snowman
description: Subject is to be kept at -130 Celsius or lower at all times. Its hat must remain on its head at all times.

name: Skink
description: Extremophile radioactive organism.

name: Yeti
description: A hulking, furred creature that can only live in extremely cold environments.

name: The Steam Engine
description: Refers to both the Train and the Anteverse (Anteverse 11) it is contained within. Should the train stop for any reason, the results could be devastating.

name: Crystallisk
description: Bipedal spider-like entities with cloaking capabilities. Incredibly fast and incredibly deadly, but with a fragility that makes conflicts quick.

name: Reservoir Growth
description: Originates from Anteverse 13.

name: The Residue
description: A highly radioactive residue of an area's time spent lost in a perforational limbo. First appeared when Gatekeepers installed an aura lock on Shadowgate.

name: Entropy Flower
description: Grows at a rapid rate due to the low entropy conditions of our universe. Originates from Anteverse 2.

name: The Paperweight
description: Weighs three times heavier when held upside-down. Believed to be in possession of The Order.

name: The Fragment
description: Has no space-time presence, as well as no mass.

name: Carbuncle
description: Starfish-like creatures that pull in their targets using a long tongue. 1st stage of the A2 metamorphic line. Originates from Anteverse 2.

name: Mushroom Carbuncle
description: A variant of IS-0171 that is infected with fungal spores.

name: Pre-Human Artifact
description: Carbon dated to ~1.2 million years ago. No observable effects. Unknown non-human origin.

name: Pest
description: Limbless creatures that leap at targets. 2nd stage of the A2 metamorphic line. Originates from Anteverse 2.

name: Electro-Pest
description: Cybernetically augmented specimens of IS-0173 that are the result of Dr. Stern's experiments.

name: Volatile Pest
description: A variant of IS-0173 that has been mutated by radiation. Explodes on high-velocity impacts.

name: The Perforations
description: Method of information transfer from Anteverse 2. No known defense.

name: Peccary
description: Quadrupedal hound-like creatures. Aggressive melee attackers. 3rd stage of the A2 metamorphic line. Originates from Anteverse 2.

name: Mushroom Peccary
description: A variant of IS-0178 that is infected with fungal spores.

name: Exor
description: Bipedal creatures with ranged combat potential. A projectile weapon seems to replace their right hand. Capable of firing 'quills' at lethal speeds. 4th stage of the A2 metamorphic line. Originates from Anteverse 2.

name: Exor Monk
description: A variant of IS-0184 that possesses the ability to teleport and fire psionic blasts instead of quills.

name: Armored Exor
description: A variant of IS-0184-A with additional armor plating.

name: Exor Cha
description: Similar in abilities to IS-0184-B, with the addition of a weapon resembling a spear.

name: Volatile Exor
description: A variant of IS-0184 that has been mutated by radiation. Has lost all ranged combat ability in favor of increased aggression.

name: [REDACTED]
description: The skeletal remains of an anomalous creature found frozen in the ice in the Swiss Alps. Guarded by IS-0134.

name: Inkfish
description: An aquatic inhabitant of Anteverse 39. Discovered in a pool of dark, inky water.

name: The Coworker
description: All staff are directed to treat IS-0223, if encountered, as a legitimate employee. Do not question or interrogate the subject. Provide with food if requested.

name: Tape Wisp
description: Entity discovered in VHS Tapes of animated television series. Believed to subsist on high-contrast magnetic recordings.

name: The Moving Box
description: The entity will store items placed within it for an indefinite period. Its inner storage capacity is known to exceed its outer dimensions, suggesting non-Euclidean internal workings.

name: Bigfoot
description: A hulking, furred creature that is similar to IS-0134.

name: Nocturnal Anomaly
description: A floating ball of fire resembling some depictions of a 'Will-o'-the-wisp'. Discovered shortly before the attack on the Cascade Facility.

name: The Game Sprite
description: A living virus that infects and spreads itself across devices.

name: Space Lettuce
description: A plant from Anteverse 21 with mushroom-like qualities and 'leaves' similar to lettuce. Remarkably edible.

name: Symphonist
description: A smaller, more agile "cousin" of IS-0064. Lacks any sense of sight.

name: Perforation Bees
description: Hives of bee-like creatures.

name: Bogman
description: A aggressive, semi-amphibious creature, believed to have a sensitivity to subsonic sound.

name: The Hate-Inducing Table
description: A table very generic in appearance, but that inflicts a severe feeling of hatred upon a subject visually perceiving it.

name: The Presence
description: No research is permitted. Staff are prohibited from entering the area.

name: The World in the Mirror
description: A giant mirror which acts as an entrance to Anteverse 36, an endless and flat Portal World of grassy fields.

name: Yellow Paint
description: A can of incredibly volatile and unpredictable yellow paint. Exact methods of containment are still unknown.

name: The Core
description: An extremely old perforation fluctuator.
